channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A Bulgarian shipping company denies its vessel sabotaged a Baltic Sea cable
item.description: SOFIA, Bulgaria (AP) — A Bulgarian shipping company on Monday denied that one of its ships had intentionally damaged an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island of Gotland.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Graham: Senate needs to investigate Trump decision to pull Bolton’s security
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) says the Senate needs to investigate President Trump’s decision to end his former national security adviser John Bolton’s personal security protection.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Preliminary report on South Korean plane crash confirms bird strike, but no cause yet
item.description: The preliminary accident report released by South Korea's Aviation and Railway Accident Investigation Board on Monday said that feathers and bird blood stains were found in both engines.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-jin Kim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Gates: 'Foolish' to spend time with Jeffrey Epstein
item.description: Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates addressed his relationship with convicted pedophile Jeffrey Epstein, who died in 2019 when he hanged himself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to reinstate service members discharged for refusing COVID vaccine and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LIVE: Auschwitz memorial marks 80th anniversary of the death camp's liberation
item.description: Nazi German forces murdered some 1.1 million people at the site in southern Poland, which was under German occupation during World War II.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Southern California rain helps firefighters but creates risk of flooding and toxic ash runoff
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Rain fell on parts of Southern California on Sunday and the scattered showers were expected to continue overnight, boosting the risk of toxic ash runoff in areas scorched by Los Angeles-area wildfires. Flood watches were in effect through 4 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arrest quotas for ICE officers 'a bad idea': William Enyart
item.description: Enacting a daily arrest quota for ICE agents would be "asking for an abuse of their police powers," Ret. U.S. Army Major Gen. William Enyart said.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — After a half-century of public silence, a freelance photographer from Vietnam has asserted he took one of the most renowned and impactful photos of the 20th century — the image of a naked girl fleeing a napalm attack in South Vietnam that has long been credited to a staff phot
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penske's pair of Porsches dominate as The Captain wins Rolex 24 at Daytona for 2nd consecutive year
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Roger Penske started his 59th season in motorsports the same way he closed last year — as a champion.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Your wages will stretch the furthest in these major US metros, study finds
item.description: A new study by finance site Bankrate found that, after adjusting for the cost of living, the average worker's buying power only grew (compared to the other cities) in a handful of large metros.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Former NBA star marks two years of his cannabis shop in St. Louis
item.description: There was celebration and appreciation in downtown St. Louis Friday as former NBA star Al Harrington marked the two-year anniversary of Viola Cannabis Dispensary.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Harris, KRON4

item.title: White House fires some independent inspectors general
item.description: NewsNation partner The Hill reported that Trump has ousted around 17 federal watchdogs within various departments
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tattoo removal expert says 1 in 7 clients asks to remove this specific tattoo
item.description: Today’s tattoo-removal experts are quite adept at ridding their clients of all types of ill-conceived tattoos. But there are a few specific categories of ill-conceived ink that they tend to treat more often than others.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: As schools in LA reopen, parents worry about harmful ash from wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Kelli Ferrone wiped blinds and window sills inside her daughters’ elementary school, just a half mile from the devastating Palisades Fire that scorched the coastal enclave in Los Angeles. The cotton swabs and wipes came up black. There is scattered ash and soot at her feet.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mexico refuses Trump admin. migrant deportation flights, sources say
item.description: U.S. military aircraft carried out two similar flights, each with about 80 migrants, to Guatemala on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Lynn Ban, star of Netflix reality show, dies at 52 after emergency surgery
item.description: Lynn Ban, who starred in Netflix's reality show "Bling Empire: New York," has died weeks after a skiing accident and emergency brain surgery.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KTLA 5

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: President Trump is reversing his predecessor’s efforts to clean up areas hardest hit by pollution from heavy industry, ports and roadways – communities that are often largely Black, Latino and low-income.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Alexa St. John, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about Trump's first executive actions on climate and environment
item.description: President Trump’s first week in office included a flurry of executive orders with implications for Earth’s climate and environment.
item.dc:creator: Melina Walling, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Brawl over Pokémon cards breaks out at California Costco
item.description: The fight, captured on video by X user DisguisedToast, unfolded on Jan. 16 at the Costco in Atwater Village, the Los Angeles Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: London court clears way to extradite US mother accused of killing 2 of her children in Colorado
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A London judge on Friday rejected a U.S. mother's challenge to her extradition to face murder charges in Colorado in the deaths of two of her young children.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mississippi bill would pay bounty hunters to catch undocumented immigrants
item.description: A Mississippi District Attorney proposed new legislation on Wednesday to pay bounty hunters a reward for helping to deport undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Homeowners in Palisades Fire area angered by bank's decision
item.description: With the devastating Palisades Fire still smoldering in California, Lisa Pelton and some of her neighbors in Mandeville Canyon received an unpleasant notice from their bank: Their home equity lines of credit were being slashed.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Activists to 'resist' Trump through know-your-rights campaign
item.description: The pulse of El Paso’s immigrant community can be felt through the calls streaming into a small white “BNHR” building on Piedras Street over the past few days.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: 7 police officers wounded in Texas shooting
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Seven San Antonio police officers were shot Wednesday night while responding to a “suicide in progress” call and a heavy presence of police were near an apartment complex trying to detain the suspect, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
